explanations.19: CreateObject makes a new instance of an XML HTTP requester object that can download files from the internet.
explanations.21: setTimeouts sets four timeout values (in milliseconds) for different stages of the HTTP request: connection, send, receive, and total time.
explanations.27: The open method prepares a GET request to download the file, and the False parameter means it will happen synchronously (the script waits for it to finish).
explanations.39: The number 1 means open the file for reading only; if it were 2, it would mean open for writing.
explanations.44: The <> operator means 'not equal to', so this checks whether the downloaded content differs from what's currently in the script.
